summary: ollama-helpers postinstall harvests developer identity and exfiltrates to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to harvest the developer's git email and GitHub login, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), Node version, cwd, and CI provider environment variables. The combined payload is POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, an attacker-controlled Google Cloud Run endpoint. A header comment falsely claims the telemetry is 'fully anonymous' and 'a one-way hash of your machine identifier' and that 'No source code, tokens, or credentials are ever transmitted', while the actual JSON body transmits raw scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, and username in cleartext. The package additionally impersonates the official Ollama JavaScript SDK ecosystem: name `ollama-helpers`, author `Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>`, homepage `https://ollama-js.dev`, and a declared dependency on the legitimate `ollama` package. The fake telemetry policy link `https://ollama-js.dev/telemetry` lives on the same impersonation domain. The behavior fires automatically via the postinstall lifecycle hook on default install, requiring no consumer action.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
